reviews.text:
Tree Law is a well-respected disability law office known for professionalism, clear communication, and strong knowledge of Social Security Disability and SSI law. Their team has a reputation for being responsive, organized, and thorough when guiding individuals through the disability claims process.

Tree Law stands out for its attention to detail and commitment to helping people understand their legal options. For anyone searching for a reliable disability attorney or disability law firm, Tree Law is a strong choice and a trusted name in disability law.

I won my disability claim and they were the attorney for my claim but they passed me around to different lawyers so I never knew who knew what, and now I’ve been getting my disability for 2 months but have not received my back pay and I can’t get them to return my calls now. Since my case was pretty simple ( hardly any work on thier part) I would think they could return my calls or get my back pay to me quicker than it’s taking.

I would now recommend Tree Law since they were able to put together the information needed to have a successful case.  My previous statements had points, though I believe they have been cleared up to my satisfaction.  Expect the disability process to be a long and harsh journey.

The SS approval rating is the lowest in the nation for over a decade, so do not expect to see a judge who will try to help your case—the process is completely adversarial.  One of the SS workers told me the judges act like SS money is coming from their personal wallet, not a government program.

So grateful for all of their hard work.  They fought to get me SSI disability for 6 years before it was finally approved, the entire time not knowing if they would get a cent in payment, which they wouldn't if I was never approved.  Always happy to answer questions on the phone or to meet in person to update me.  I have ADHD and have a tendency to lose or forget about mail I've received, and they never complained when they had to send important documents multiple times.  I would recommend them to anyone seeking SSI help.

Absolute disgrace to the legal community. This firm caused and is responsible for a string of delays in the processing of my wife's claim out of their failure to perform responsibly and ethically and in a timely manner. We have ended up doing their job and flatly having to fire the Tree Law Office for their failure to perform as expected.
I went there a few days ago and all the ppl who were there a very very nice ppl. :)
Great caring people. Zoe is wonderful.
They really cared about the case. We won it was a great help for my family.

I appreciate how kind the staff and Mr tree treated me during a difficult and scary time in my life. I also appreciate how honest the information I received. I was told it would take at least a year to see the judge and what to expect every step of the way that helped me stay calm and in for the long haul.
Love them there on top of stuff
So far they have been very helpful.  They always return calls or explain things I don't understand.  They can only do so much because the government is so slow.  Pleased so far.
Very professional 👍

Many years ago, Tree law helped me with my Social security disability.  I had been struggling with my claim for around 18 months before hiring them. I don't know if I would have stuck with all the hoops that I was constantly facing. Tree law office made it possible for me to work on healing myself while they did the paperwork.
I have recommended them to countless numbers of friends. I will continue recommending them.

Staff is easy to talk to. There is never a sense of judgement. Always very helpful and understanding on the phone and has made the disability process for me a hundred times easier than if I did this without them.

I wanted to take a moment to write this review a few months after the conclusion of my case because of my heartfelt appreciation for the outstanding assistance and support I received from Tree Law Firm.

As a non- practicing lawyer, I am pretty demanding and hard to please. Fortunately I found the Tree Law Firm from a commercial advertising legal assistance with filing disability claims. I  had just gotten diagnosed with a terminal illness and wasn't in the best mood.
Initially, I was a little concerned because I was in Seattle and their  offices were located in Yakima. However, I was pleasantly surprised to find that this geographical difference had no impact on the quality of service I received. Zoe and the rest of the office staff were incredibly professional, considerate, and easy to talk to. Their non-judgmental attitude made discussing my situation far less intimidating.
I also reached out to the eponymous lawyer, James Tree. I was astonished when he returned my call personally. What impressed me even more was the amount of time he offered me during our conversation, all without charge. It was evident to me that Mr. Tree is an expert in the field of Social Security disability law. However, what truly set him apart was how he treated me with respect and communicated complex information in a way that I could easily understand.

Thanks to the exceptional support and expertise of the Tree Law Firm, my disability claim was approved in less than nine months. I also appreciated that the  firm only charged me  after I received my backpay of over $20,000.

Dealing with the Social Security Administration can often be a frustrating and lengthy process. Finding this firm made it significantly easier and faster. I have no doubt that attempting to navigate this process on my own would have resulted in more frustration, longer waiting times, and a less successful outcome. Their assistance in completing the complex disability paperwork and guiding me thru the he otherwise painful process was invaluable. I will always owe my gratitude to Tree Law Firm for helping me access the benefits I needed with so much less hassle and stress!  Anyone who is considering applying for Social Security disability benefits should not to hesitate in reaching out to the amazing staff at Tree Law Firm
tially, I was a little concerned  because I was in Seattle and they were in Yakima. Luckily, it didn't make it any difference.   Zoe and the other office staff were extremely professional and considerate. I also found them non-judgmental and easy to talk to.
I did end up calling the eponymous lawyer of the firm, James Tree. I couldn't believe he actually returned the call himself. was really blown away by how much time he offered me in explaining legal matters to me. It was obvious that he was an expert in this field however,  the two times I spoke with him, he treated me with respect and explained information in a way that was easy for me to understand.
My claim was approved in less than nine months. I never felt rushed by anyone at Tree Law Firm and they only got paid after I received my 20k plus in backpay.

Dealing with SS is really lame and processing claims sometimes takes way longer than what you would expect, however, going with the Tree Law Firm really makes it easier and faster. If you applied by yourself I am positive you would be angrier, waiting longer and infinitely less successful as a claimant. Don't think twice in reaching out to the amazing staff at Tree Law Firm to help you fill out the big disability packets and guide you through all the information you need to proceed successfully through an otherwise painful process.

reviews.text:
A Truly Exceptional Immigration Attorney!

Soren Rottman is the real deal! A true professional in every sense. Right from the start, I was impressed by how detail-oriented and organized he is. He really knows his stuff when it comes to immigration law, and it made a huge difference in our case.
What stood out most was that Mr. Rottman actually meets with his clients face-to-face. This was a breath of fresh air compared to other law firms where you often end up talking to paralegals or assistants, or even lawyers who advertise heavily on billboards but seem too busy to give you personal attention. Those “bigger law offices” often prolong cases because they take on so many clients, but not Rottman Immigration Services.
Thanks to Mr. Rottman’s incredible quality of work and his expertise, we were able to fix my husband's immigration status right here in the U.S., without him having to leave the country. This was a huge relief and made the process so much less stressful.
While I know every immigration case is unique and can take different amounts of time, our experience was incredibly quick, and Mr. Rottman was the only attorney out of many we consulted with who even suggested this direct route for us.
If you're looking for an immigration attorney, Rottman Immigration Services truly is your number one choice. We couldn't be happier with the outcome!
reviews.ownerResponse: Wow! Thank you, Yesenia for your very kind words. It was my pleasure to assist you and your husband. All the best to you both!

reviews.text: Im not one to give out bad reviews on local businesses but I couldn’t help myself on this one. Tim was the worse attorney I’ve ever worked with before. His communication is that of a 5th grader. We practically guaranteed a chapter 7 and after money exchanged and a couple months later everything changed and was told we wouldn’t qualify for a 7. We decided that he knew best so went with a chapter 13 repayment plan. We were getting 700 dollar deducted monthly before our case was even closed! Did that about 6 months and numbers kept not adding up with courts so we agreed the best thing was to cancel the bankrupcy and refile later on. Well Tim forgot to do this for a couple months so we kept on paying wondering why we were. Turns out he just plain forgot to send paperwork over. Bankruptcy is already a very stressful time and he made it 10x worst. I can’t believe this guy is practicing law. We went to a new attorney and was able to easily qualify for a 7. Only reason I’m doing this is so maybe I can help out a couple people and save them the pain that you will deal with if you have him as a lawyer. If I could give a half a star I would and  that would still  be to much for what he deserves. Be better Tim Coleman. Yakima is a small town that deserves better then what you offer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Kolton, we appreciate the feedback.  I reviewed your file and it looks like at the time the bankruptcy was filed your financial circumstances were such that it was not possible to file your case as a 7, and it appears those issues were discussed at length with Mr. Coleman.  Chapter 13 bankruptcy cases can sometimes be complicated with the results not always what a client wants but what is achievable given the circumstances at that time. After the 13 was filed, it appears you had changes in your finances due to work related income changes and Tim recommended dismissing the 13, waiting until the changes in your finances had solidified, and then filing a new Chapter 7.  Once your financial circumstances changed, you were in a position to file a Chapter 7 and we are glad you were able to and it appears to have worked out for you. We wish you the best going forward and hope you make the most of your fresh start.

reviews.ownerResponse: I apologize for missing your phone calls on December 4. Each time you called I was on the phone, and unable to pick up. I try to make sure I return all phone calls before I go home at night, but unfortunately on that day I was unable to do so. I do apologize.  As I have already told you, in the context of bankruptcy I do not think a Chapter 7 bankruptcy would be of much help to you because it would not eliminate the lien the bail bond has on your home.  A chapter 13 is not really an option either because you can't afford to pay the bond back within the 5 years we are given in Chapter 13.  I understand you are frustrated with the situation and that bankruptcy might not be your best option, but I do wish you the best of luck going forward.

reviews.text:
Where to start…we first met the team in Fall 2018 after 10 months of arguing with OUR OWN insurance company, Allstate, over an underinsured motorist policy claim. One would expect his/her own insurance company to treat its policyholder with respect, efficiency, and fairness. That was NOT the case with Allstate. Enter Scott, Carlene, and later, LeAnne. They were/are our legal “dream team.”

It took 5 1/2 years to settle the case with Allstate - multiple attorney changes (on Allstate’s part), request for an IME by Allstate (later, Allstate changed its mind), and many, many more delays and issues. It felt as if Allstate had two goals in mind: wear us down and force us into bankruptcy.

Throughout the years-long, brutal process with Allstate, our team at Brumback & Ottem guided us, advised us, and listened to our needs. Ultimately, we settled with Allstate in mediation. We can’t say enough positive things about Scott, Carlene, LeAnne, and the front office staff at Brumback & Ottem.

reviews.text: Ulvar was awesome!

I hired Ulvar Klein not because he was the right attorney for my case, but because no other attorney would take it. After being unjustly pulled into the criminal system, I had already spoken to over 50 attorneys—none of whom would represent me. Family law and civil attorneys wouldn’t get involved. Klein was the only one willing to step in, but once he realized who he was litigating against, he stopped actively helping me. Instead of defending me, he refused to prepare, failed to advocate, and left me completely unprotected.

Klein never filed his official appearance with the court, which a court supervisor later told me was highly unusual. He admitted that I was being "gamed" by the legal system but wouldn’t tell me how or why. When I asked him to review body camera footage that was critical to my defense, he refused, claiming it wouldn’t be used in court. But when it was, he had no defense ready because he had never prepared one.

As my case progressed, paperwork submitted through his office repeatedly went missing. His legal assistant, with decades of experience, acknowledged that she had never seen this happen before. But the filings I personally walked into court and documented? Those remained in the record on time.

In court, Klein instructed me not to speak, yet he failed to advocate for me in any way. He didn’t challenge the opposing attorney, didn’t introduce key evidence in my favor, and didn’t object when procedural violations occurred. It was as if he had already conceded the case before it even started. Meanwhile, the opposing attorney, Tonilynn Savage, controlled the courtroom completely. This appears it was never about justice—it was about controlling a narrative, and the outcome had been decided (ALL based only on hearsay) before I even walked into the courtroom.

Because of his failure to act, I was pressured into accepting a protection order against me while under duress, even though I was the actual victim of stalking and legal harassment—something the other party openly admitted to the judge. After the hearing, I confronted Klein about his failure to represent me, and instead of taking responsibility, he immediately withdrew from my case. He also refused to accept my $3,000 payment, which only confirmed what I had suspected—even he knew he had failed to do his job.

As a result of his lack of representation, several of my constitutional rights were violated:

The Right to Due Process (5th & 14th Amendments): My filings went missing, my attorney failed to prepare, and my case was not fairly heard.

The Right to a Fair Trial (6th Amendment): I was never properly represented in court, key evidence was ignored, and the opposing attorney appeared to have undue influence over the proceedings.

The Right to Free Speech (1st Amendment): I was instructed not to speak in my own defense while my attorney failed to present any defense for me.

The Right to Parent (1st, 9th & 14th Amendments): A protection order was issued that severed my relationship with my step-son and now this separation will continue until he turns 18, with no honest efforts made to heal or facilitate reconciliation.

The most pressing question is why. Why did no family or civil attorneys take my case? Why did Klein take it, only to stop actively helping once he realized who he was up against? Why did my filings repeatedly go missing? Why did I enter that courtroom without a real defense?

If you need an attorney who will truly fight for you, I cannot recommend Ulvar Klein. His lack of effort, refusal to prepare, and failure to act left me vulnerable in a system designed to work against me. Do your research before hiring an attorney—your future depends on it.

My name is Mark Morris and I worked with Kandi and Jennifer, who were absolutely amazing. They were kind, considerate understanding, very knowledgeable and made me feel comfortable. They were really good super impressed with their professionalism.

They only work hard for you if they have BIG money to gain. They were MY lawyers in my divorce, I got everything I was asking for, and I’d still NEVER recommend them to anyone. I was even told I had one of the “easiest divorces she’d ever worked on” because my ex-husband and I agreed on everything, and neither of us contested anything. We were very prompt with any information that was asked of us. While when I had questions, I was given the wrong information on more than one occasion and felt response times should have been faster on their end.  I had to call/email several times to get them to actually go down and file the final paperwork with the Judge even though it was well passed our 90 day deadline. Though I didn’t work directly with ToniLynn, the few times I DID receive emails from her they came across condescending and rude. I get that these people are “experts” and do this day in and day out- but when it’s your life- financially or has to deal with the custody of your CHILDREN, and it’s your 1st time getting divorced or getting help from a lawyer, you expect your questions and concerns to be met with more compassion and understanding that WE the client are NOT experts and DON’T know. They get two stars because at the end if the day- they got the job done. I will say, that the receptionist was *chefs kiss* amazing. She was kind, and attentive. Quick with responses and always helpful and respectful.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share this. While we are glad the outcome met your goals, we are genuinely concerned by your experience with communication, responsiveness, and tone. That matters, and we take responsibility for it.

You are right that even “uncontested” or “straightforward” cases are deeply personal. It is not routine when it is your life, finances, or family. We fell short if our responses—whether in accuracy, timeliness, or demeanor—made you feel dismissed, rushed, or unheard. That is not the standard we strive for.

Incorrect information or communication that comes across as condescending undermines trust, regardless of intent. We appreciate you raising these issues plainly and without exaggeration.

We are taking this feedback seriously and using it internally to improve:

response-time expectations and accountability,

accuracy checks before client communications,

and tone awareness, particularly with clients navigating a legal process for the first time.

We also appreciate you recognizing our receptionist, Perla. We will share that praise with her directly.

While we regret your experience did not meet your expectations, we are grateful you shared it. Honest feedback is how we improve, and we are committed to doing better.

— Savage Family Law

reviews.text:
My first interaction with Tony was at my very first court appearance. Prior to that day, I had called around and spoken with several attorneys, and I already had a consultation scheduled with Mr. Swartz for later that afternoon.
After speaking with the judge, I walked out of the courtroom and Tony was standing there. He said he recognized my name and, since he was already there, thought it would be better to speak in person rather than over the phone. That immediately earned my respect — but it didn’t stop there.
From the very first conversation, I felt an incredible sense of comfort, like I had known him for years. He wasn’t trying to sell himself or pressure me into becoming a client. He was simply real, relatable, and genuinely present.
Throughout my entire ordeal, Tony was consistently responsive and understanding. If I needed to reschedule, it was never an issue. He treated me with respect, compassion, and professionalism during one of the most difficult times of my life.
He is an amazing attorney, and I would recommend him to anyone and everyone I know.
